_source: nfa0 = China net IIP / nominal GDP, end-2025. REAL data: SAFE (State Administration of Foreign Exchange) 'China's International Investment Position as at the End of 2025' — net external assets USD 4,071.3 billion; NBS official 2025 nominal GDP 140.1879 trillion yuan = USD 20.01 trillion (gov.cn, implied CNY/USD 7.006). 4071.3 / 20010 = +20.35% of GDP. Cross-check: BoFiT end-June 2025 implied ~20% of GDP. China is a net creditor. UNIT FACTORS (units.*): fxToUsd=6.7686 is the REAL CNY/USD spot rate, FRED DEXCHUS latest non-missing observation 2026-06-18 (api.stlouisfed.org/fred, series DEXCHUS); NOT synthetic.
_flags: iNFA (imputed return on NFA, ~3% p.a.) and fxShare (FX-exposed NFA share, ~0.4) are MODELING DEFAULTS shipped flagged (like the structural SFA), NOT estimated/sourced.
_unit_factors_note: units.* convert the unit-mismatched native level paths to a single common NOMINAL-USD scale so the TB/GDP (and CA, NFA roll-forward) ratio is comparable: gdpScale=1e6 (GDP_CN is in MILLIONS of CNY → ones), fxToUsd=6.7686 (CNY/USD spot, FRED DEXCHUS 2026-06-18 — REAL, sourced; GDP-in-CNY ÷ this = GDP-in-USD), tradeFreqMult=3 (EXP_CN/IMP_CN are MONTHLY-average USD flows vs a QUARTERLY GDP → ×3 to quarterize), gdpKind='nominalLocal' (documents GDP_CN is nominal CNY, mirrors idx.gdpIsNominal). These are pure unit conversions, not fabricated data; each native series remains its real sourced value. Sanity: 100·3·($3.15e11−$2.40e11)/(3.6e7·1e6/6.7686) ≈ +4% of GDP, matching China's published goods-trade surplus (~4-5% of GDP). externalChartable now TRUE — CN renders external charts on the common-USD ratio. See app/src/lib/compute/externalAccounting.ts (ExternalUnitFactors) and app/src/__tests__/compute/externalAccounting.test.ts.
